description.en: An important rain-fed upland food crop grown in the Dry Zone and Intermediate Zone of Sri Lanka. Hardy, drought-tolerant, and cultivatable under adverse soil and climatic conditions. Average yields are modest but improvable with better agronomy.

rainfall_mm.quote: can be cultivated where it receives an average rainfall of 500 mm per year

temperature_c.quote: optimal average growth temperature range is 18-27°C but it can withstand up to 36°C

expected_yield_kg_per_acre.quote: yields are modest (around 1.0–1.3 t/ha on average)

description.quote: Finger millet is an important food crop grown in rain-fed uplands in the Dry zone and Intermediate zone of Sri Lanka... can be cultivated under adverse soil and climatic conditions
